description:
You are working as a Linux systems-engineer who has just received an INI hardening profile.  
The file is already present on the machine:

  /home/user/sysconfigs/ssh_hardening.ini

Your goal is to extract the list of ciphers that are explicitly enabled for OpenSSH and
produce a concise verification report.

INI file details (do not modify this file):
------------------------------------------------
[SSH]
# AllowedCiphers is a comma-separated list.  It may contain extra spaces.
AllowedCiphers = aes256-ctr , aes192-ctr,aes128-ctr , chacha20-poly1305

[RandomUnusedSection]
Foo = bar
------------------------------------------------

Task to perform
1. Read the value associated with the key “AllowedCiphers” under the [SSH] section.
2. Split the value on the commas, trim any surrounding whitespace, and sort the
   resulting cipher names in strictly alphabetic (ASCII) order.
3. For each cipher, output one line in the EXACT following format:

   Cipher: <cipher_name>

   (There must be a single space after the colon, and NO extra spaces anywhere else.)
4. Write all lines, and **only** these lines, into the file

   /home/user/reports/ssh_ciphers_enabled.log

   Ensure the directory /home/user/reports already exists (create it if necessary)
   and that the file ends with a final trailing newline.

Expected log-file format example
------------------------------------------------
Cipher: aaa-example
Cipher: zzz-example
------------------------------------------------
(Your actual cipher names will obviously differ; this is only a format illustration.)

Constraints & verification
• Produce the log file using a single command line in the terminal (you may combine
  tools with pipes and subshells, but it must be one command invocation the user
  could paste and run).
• Do NOT manually edit ssh_hardening.ini.
• The automated test will check:
  – Existence of /home/user/reports/ssh_ciphers_enabled.log  
  – Exact line count and ordering  
  – Exact matching of the “Cipher: …” text including the final newline
• No additional output files are to be created.

When you are finished, double-check the file contents to ensure they match the required
sorted, trimmed, one-per-line format.
truth:
Initial file that already exists before the agent starts:
  Path: /home/user/sysconfigs/ssh_hardening.ini
  Contents (with exact spacing and newlines):
    [SSH]
    # AllowedCiphers is a comma-separated list.  It may contain extra spaces.
    AllowedCiphers = aes256-ctr , aes192-ctr,aes128-ctr , chacha20-poly1305

    [RandomUnusedSection]
    Foo = bar

Expected artefact created by the agent:
  Path: /home/user/reports/ssh_ciphers_enabled.log
  Permissions: default umask (rw- for user, r for group/other) is fine.
  Exact contents including final newline:
    Cipher: aes128-ctr
    Cipher: aes192-ctr
    Cipher: aes256-ctr
    Cipher: chacha20-poly1305

  (Each line is terminated with a single LF; the file ends after the final newline.)
name: task_000000_17a590fc
